definition: A municipality in Almería, Andalusia, Spain.
A Tamil caste of southern India and Sri Lanka.
The thin, horny plate at the ends of fingers and toes on humans and some other animals.
Lacking worldly experience, wisdom, or judgement; unsophisticated.
Any nounal word or phrase which indicates a particular person, place, class, or thing.
Synonym of maid: a servant girl.
To have a nap; to sleep for a short period of time, especially during the day.
A serviette; a (usually rectangular) piece of cloth or paper used at the table for wiping the mouth and hands for cleanliness while eating.
Any of several bulbous flowering plants, of the genus Narcissus, having white or yellow cup- or trumpet-shaped flowers, notably the daffodil

Any substance or drug that reduces pain, induces sleep and may alter mood or behaviour; in some contexts, especially in reference to the opiates-and-opioids class, especially in reference to illegal drugs, and often both.
To relate (a story or series of events) in speech or writing.
The act of recounting or relating in order the particulars of some action, occurrence, or affair; a narrating.
One who narrates or tells stories.
Having a small width; not wide; having opposite edges or sides that are close, especially by comparison to length or depth.
In a narrow manner; without flexibility or latitude.
Of or pertaining to the nose or to the nasion.
A historically constituted, stable community of people, formed based on a common language, territory, economic life, ethnicity and/or psychological make-up manifested in a common culture.
Pertaining to a nation or country, especially as a whole; affecting, shared by, or existing throughout all of a nation.
A more extreme form of patriotism; the idea of a more extreme support for one's country, people or culture.
Of, relating to, or advocating nationalism.
Legal membership of a particular nation or state, by origin, birth, naturalization, ownership, allegiance or otherwise.
To make into, or to become, a nation.

The way things are, the totality of all things in the physical universe and their order, especially the physical world in contrast to spiritual realms and flora and fauna as distinct from human conventions, art, and technology.
Mischievous; tending to misbehave or act badly (especially of a child).
A feeling of illness or discomfort in the digestive system, usually characterized by a strong urge to vomit.
Of or relating to a navy.
The middle or body of a church, extending from the transepts to the principal entrances.
To plan, control and record the position and course of a vehicle, ship, aircraft, etc., on a journey; to follow a planned course.
The theory, practice and technology of charting a course for a road vehicle, ship, aircraft, or spaceship.
A person who navigates, especially an officer with that responsibility on a ship or an aircrew member with that responsibility on an aircraft.
A country's entire maritime military force, including ships and personnel.
Abbreviation of Nebraska: a state of the United States.
Clean, tidy; free from dirt or impurities.
Required, essential, whether logically inescapable or needed in order to achieve a desired result or avoid some penalty.
To make necessary; to behove; to require (something) to be brought about.
The quality or state of being necessary, unavoidable, or absolutely requisite.
The part of the body connecting the head and the trunk found in humans and some animals.
An article of jewelry that is worn around the neck, most often made of a string of precious metal, pearls, gems, beads or shells, and sometimes having a pendant attached.
A strip of cloth worn around the neck and tied in the front.
A requirement for something; something needed.

A long, thin, sharp implement usually for piercing as in sewing, embroidery, acupuncture, tattooing, body piercing, medical injections, sutures, etc; or a blunt but otherwise similar implement used for forming loops or knots in crafts such as darning, knitting, tatting, etc.
Either directly or indirectly preceded or followed by an auxiliary verb, often must: of necessity or need; necessarily, indispensably.
To deny the existence, evidence, or truth of; to contradict.
Not positive or neutral; bad; undesirable; unfavourable.
To fail to care for or attend to something.
Tending to neglect; failing to take care of matters which require attention; forgetful.
Able to be neglected, ignored or excluded from consideration; too small or unimportant to be of concern.
To confer with others in order to come to terms or reach an agreement.
The process of achieving agreement through discussion.
An island of the Philippines, located in the Visayas.
The residential area near one's home.
Situated or living nearby or adjacent to.
Not one of two; not either; not one or the other.
The chemical element (symbol Ne) with an atomic number of 10. The second of the noble gases, it is a colourless, odorless inert gas.
A son of one's sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either a son of one's brother (fraternal nephew) or a son of one's sister (sororal nephew).
Roman Emperor from 54 to 68, and the last Emperor of the Julio-Claudian dynasty.
A bundle of neurons with their connective tissue sheaths, blood vessels and lymphatics.
A female given name, nickname of Vanessa.
A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.
To settle oneself comfortably and snugly.
A mesh of string, cord or rope.
Any plant whose foliage is covered with stinging, mildly poisonous hairs, causing an instant rash.
Of, or relating to the nerves, neurons or the nervous system.
A cell of the nervous system, which conducts nerve impulses; consisting of an axon and several dendrites. Neurons are connected by synapses.

A mental disorder, less severe than psychosis, marked by anxiety or fear which differ from normal measures by their intensity, which disorder results from a failure to compromise or properly adjust during the developmental stages of life, between normal human instinctual impulses and the demands of human society.
Not taking sides in a conflict such as war; nonaligned.
The state or quality of being neutral; the condition of being unengaged in contests between others; state of taking no part on either side.
To make (something) even, inactive or ineffective.
A subatomic particle forming part of the nucleus of an atom and having no charge; it is a combination of an up quark and two down quarks.
At no time; on no occasion; in no circumstance.
In spite of what preceded; yet.
One who has recently come to a community; a recent arrival.
Very recently/lately; in the immediate past.
A retail business selling newspapers, magazines, and stationery; a stationer.
One who delivers the news for broadcast on television, radio, etc; a newsreader.
A periodically sent publication containing current events or the like, generally on a particular topic or geared toward a limited audience.

A publication, usually published daily or weekly, containing news and other articles. Traditionally a print publication typically printed on cheap, low-quality paper; today usually digital and often also available in print.
Nearest in place or position, having nothing similar intervening; adjoining.
To take a small, quick bite, or several of such bites, of (something).
A cavity, hollow, or recess, generally within the thickness of a wall, for a statue, bust, or other erect ornament.
A small cut in a surface.
A silvery elemental metal with an atomic number of 28 and symbol Ni.
A familiar, invented name for a person or thing used instead of the actual name of the person or thing, often based on some noteworthy characteristic.
A daughter of one’s sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either the daughter of one's brother ("fraternal niece"), or of one's sister ("sororal niece").
totally silent, as a musical direction (abbreviated to n. or ∅ in sheet music)
The time when the Sun is below the horizon when the sky is dark.
A public or private establishment that is open late at night to provide entertainment, food, drink, music or dancing.
A nightgown; (usually female) attire designed to be worn to bed.
The close of the day; the coming of night.
A long loose robe worn mainly by women for sleeping in.
Happening or appearing in the night; night-time; nocturnal.
A very unpleasant or frightening dream.
At night (during night-time, especially on a regular basis).
A numerical value equal to 9; the number following eight and preceding ten.
The cardinal number occurring after eighteen and before twenty, represented in Roman numerals as XIX and in Arabic numerals as 19. It is the last/largest of the "teens".
The ordinal form of the number nineteen.
The ordinal form of the number ninety.
The cardinal number occurring after eighty-nine and before ninety-one, represented in Roman numerals as XC and in Arabic numerals as 90.
The ordinal form of the number nine.
To catch and enclose or compress tightly between two surfaces, or points which are brought together or closed; to pinch; to close in upon.
The projection of a mammary gland from which, on female therian mammals, milk is secreted.
Any salt or ester of nitric acid.
The chemical element with an atomic number of 7 and atomic weight of 14.0067. It is a colorless and odorless gas.
A noble or privileged social class, historically accompanied by a hereditary title; aristocracy.
An aristocrat; one of aristocratic blood.
A peer; an aristocrat; ranks range from baron to king to emperor.
Not any person; the logical negation of somebody.
To incline the head up and down, as to indicate agreement.
A knot, knob, protuberance or swelling.
Of or pertaining to film noir, or the atmosphere associated with that genre
Various sounds, usually unwanted or unpleasant.
in a noisy manner; in such a way as to create a great deal of noise or sound
Making a noise, especially a loud unpleasant sound
A member of a society or class who herd animals from pasture to pasture with no fixed home.
A prefecture or unit of regional government in Greece.
Of, resembling, relating to, or consisting of a name or names.
To name someone as a candidate for a particular role or position, including that of an office.
An act or instance of nominating.
A person named, or designated, by another, to any office, duty, or position; one nominated, or proposed, by others for office or for election to office.
Not any of a given number or group.
Letters or words, in writing or speech, that have no meaning or pattern or seem to have no meaning.
Without stopping; without interruption or break.
A string or flat strip of pasta or other dough, usually cooked (at least initially) by boiling, and served in soup or in a dry form mixed with a sauce and other ingredients.
A small corner formed by two walls; an alcove.
The time of day when the Sun seems to reach its highest point in the sky; solar noon.
The time of noon; the time of day when the sun is highest in the sky, especially on a hot day.
And... not (introducing a negative statement, without necessarily following one).
That which is normal or typical.
According to norms or rules or to a regular pattern.
A normal espresso drink, neither ristretto (shorter) nor lungo (longer).
Under normal conditions or circumstances; usually; most of the time
A member of the Norteño Mexican gang.

The direction towards the pole to the left-hand side of someone facing east, specifically 0°, or (on another celestial object) the direction towards the pole lying on the northern side of the invariable plane.
The intercardinal compass point halfway between north and east; specifically at a bearing of 45°.
Of, related to, located in, or from the northeast.
Of, facing, situated in, or related to the north; northerly.
The direction or area lying to the north of a place.
The intercardinal compass point halfway between west and north; specifically at a bearing of 315°.
Of or pertaining to the northwest; from or to in such a direction.
A protuberance on the face housing the nostrils, which are used to breathe or smell.
A longing for home or familiar surroundings; homesickness.
Either of the two orifices located on the nose (or on the beak of a bird); used as a passage for air and other gases to travel the nasal passages.
Negates the meaning of the modified verb.
Worthy of note; remarkable; memorable; noted or distinguished.
As a pointed example; in a notable manner.
A book (physical or digital) in which notes or memoranda are written.
Well known because of one's reputation; famous, celebrated.
Deserving attention; notable; worthy of notice.
Capable of being seen or noticed.
Mental apprehension of whatever may be known, thought, or imagined; idea, concept.
Of, containing, or being a notion; mental or imaginary.
Ellipsis of Notre Dame De Chartres Hospital: a hospital in Baguio, Benguet, Philippines.

A word that functions as the name of a specific object or set of objects, such as person, animal, place, word, thing, phenomenon, substance, quality, or idea: one of the basic parts of speech in many languages, including English.
The act of nourishing or the state of being nourished.
The mind or intellect, reason, both rational and emotional.
Any sudden brightening of a previously inconspicuous star.
Newly made, formed or evolved; having no precedent; of recent origin; new.
The state of being new or novel; newness.
The eleventh month of the Gregorian calendar, following October and preceding December.
A beginner; one who is not very familiar or experienced in a particular subject.
At the present time; in the current era.
The letter of the Greek alphabet Ν (N) and ν (n).
Pertaining to the nucleus of an atom.
The core, central part of something, around which other elements are assembled.
Without clothing or other covering of the skin; without clothing on the genitals or female nipples.
The state or quality of being without clothing on the body; specifically, the quality of being without clothing on the genitals.
New or novel, usually in reference to Latin American culture.
A commune in Yonne department, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France.
Physically unable to feel, not having the power of sensation.
The Book of Numbers, the fourth of the Books of Moses in the Old Testament of the Bible, the fourth book in the Torah.
A symbol that is not a word and represents a number, such as the Arabic numerals 1, 2, 3 or the Roman numerals I, V, X, L.
A member of a Christian religious community of women who live by certain vows and usually wear a habit, (Roman Catholicism, specifically) those living together in a cloister.
Of or pertaining to wedding and marriage.
A person involved in providing direct care for the sick:
A place where nursing (“breastfeeding”) or the raising of children is carried on.
In the state of suckling young, lactating.
The act of nourishing or nursing; tender care
Any of various hard-shelled seeds or hard, dry fruits from various families of plants.
A source of nourishment, such as food, that can be metabolized by an organism to give energy and build tissue.
The organic process by which an organism assimilates food and uses it for growth and maintenance.
Providing nutrients; healthy to eat or drink.

Originally, the DuPont company trade name for polyamide, a copolymer whose molecules consist of alternating diamine and dicarboxylic acid monomers bonded together; now generically used for this type of polymer.
Any female nature spirit associated with water, forests, grotto, wind, etc.
Used when giving the former name of a man.
